reason: The item cleanly tests boundary-aware admissibility judgments around HF-based warning utility, SOC-dependent HF scaling, and heating-rate transfer, with correct options tightly anchored to stated comparisons or explicit caveats. Decoys are largely single-fault and plausible (scope misuse, unsupported mechanism link, and extrapolation), creating meaningful separation. Minor neutrality risk comes from one option (E) being broadly phrased (“regardless of chemistry”), which can feel stylistically more definitive than peers and slightly eases test-taking heuristics.

The item cleanly targets boundary-aware admissible inference: what can (and cannot) be concluded about CFD validity, localization of model limitations, and generalization of explosion pressures beyond the tested geometry. Decoys are largely single-fault and engineering-plausible (scope overreach, universalization from LBV, and oversimplified worst-case characterization), and the wording does not strongly telegraph correctness. Discrimination is solid but not maximal because A’s “dominant mechanism” phrasing is somewhat interpretive given the limited reported validation signals, slightly softening the epistemic boundary.
